DTEval 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Deseasonalise Diffusion Tube Data

    deseason.tube.Rd

    Functions for deseasonalising multiple year diffusion tube (DT) data time-series.

    deseasonTubeData(data, tube = ".value", by = NULL, method = 1, ...)

    Arguments

    data

    Data source, typically a data.frame or similar, containing data-series of diffusion tube records.

    tube

    The name of the data-series (in data) to fit a model to (see method and Details), typically the DT NO2 concentrations (in ug/m3).

    by

    The name(s) of hierarchical grouping terms. These are used to sub-sample the data when deseasonalising data.

    method

    The deseasonalisation method to apply: 1 (default) LOESS; 2 local LOESS, like 1 but LOESS is applied to groups of one or more near sites, see also Details.

    ...

    additional arguments, currently passed to loess.

    Value

    All functions return the supplied data.frame with attached predicted time-series components: ..season, ..deseason, etc.

    Details

    deseasonTubeData attempts to deseasonalise the supplied time-series.

    It builds a LOESS time-series model in the form:

    [tube] ~ loess([day-of-year] + [date])

    And, extracts the seasonal component as the response term for the day-of-year.

    method 1 applies the LOESS model directly at the requested by level.

    method 2 applies the LOESS model by-location in addition to the requested by level. Used directly it is equivalent to:

    deseasonTubeData(..., method=1, by=".location")

    ... or similar, but can to expanded to build near-location models using max.distance and/or max.n to set the maximum distance between and/or number of sampling sites in the subset near data used to build these models.

    Note

    deseasonTubeData and related functions assume that data is a data set DTEval will recognise as Diffusion Tube data, so either previously tagged tube data or data that is tag-able using a default call of tagTube

    Examples

    # ambient data exhibits seasonality
tubePlot(dt.brd, ".date",".value", plot.type="smooth")


# underlying trend
mod<-deseasonTubeData(dt.brd,".value", by=".location")
tubePlot(mod,".date", "..trend", plot.type="smooth")

    Contents

    Developed by Karl Ropkins.
